title: Quasar (Human)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Quasar}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Quasar.jpg]] |name=Quasar |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Star Wars (1977) 24|''Star Wars'' (1977) 24]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Obi-Wan, I'm just a peacetime space-man! I don't know anything about warfare! Will you take command?|Quasar|}} '''Quasar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who [[Captain/Legends|captained]] a commercial [[pleasure cruiser]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which transported important dignitaries and lowlifes alike. Quasar's ship once carried [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on a trip to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. On that voyage, Quasar's ship was threatened by [[Merson pirates/Legends|pirates]] in the [[Merson asteroid belt]], leading the captain to cede control of his ship to Kenobi, who fought off the raiders. ==Biography== {{Quote|What are your armaments?"
"Arm--? You must be joking, man! This is a pleasure cruiser!|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Quasar|}} A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], the man known as Quasar went into business as the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of a [[pleasure cruiser]] during the era of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Quasar operated during an era where the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] were relatively safe, and large pleasure [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] that transported beings from all walks of life between [[Planet/Legends|planets]] were common. Quasar's cruiser hosted [[Politics/Legends|politicians]], criminals, and one on occasion, a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], on his way to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. On that eventful trip, Quasar asked Kenobi to escort a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] called [[68-RKO]], which was en route to enter the service of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], as a form of recompense for the fact that Kenobi was traveling at a very cheap rate. Kenobi consented, although the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] presence caused a fight between the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and a [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|droid-hating]] [[Joey Orsel|fellow passenger]] that ended when Kenobi ran the man through with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. [[File:QuasarKenobi.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Captain Quasar and Obi-Wan Kenobi observe the Merson pirate ships following their craft.]] Soon afterward, Quasar sent for Kenobi directly, asking for the Jedi's help during a perilous stage of their journey. The cruiser's path to Alderaan took it through the [[Merson asteroid belt]], which was inhabited by [[Merson pirates/Legends|notorious pirates]] that preyed on passing [[Starship/Legends|ships]]. It was common practice for ships coming through the belt to shut down their non-essential systems and silently drift along with the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]], disguising their presence. However, on this occasion, Quasar noticed a group of four [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] ships seemingly following them, an extremely unusual occurrence. Unsure of what to do, Quasar asked for Kenobi's counsel, and soon ceded command of the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] to the Jedi when the pirates suddenly turned to attack. Quasar was amazed when Kenobi deftly fought off the pirates with just a pair of [[energy cannon]]s, before the Jedi left the bridge to investigate his suspicion that the bandits were following a signal originating from within Quasar's ship. Kenobi soon found the culprit: a microwave emitter originating from a fermentation device at the ship's bar.{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20070228/index.html|text=Time Capsule: Obi-Wan Kenobi 1979}} ==Personality and traits== Although Quasar served as the captain of his pleasure cruiser, he was not above turning to others for advice when faced with a situation he did not know how to handle, and especially respected the counsel of the Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi. And when the the situation threatened violence, as it did in the Merson asteroid belt, Quasar was willing to turn over command of his ship entirely, as he had absolutely no schooling or experience in warfare. ==Behind the scenes== Quasar made his first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in [[Star Wars (1977) 24|''Star Wars'' (1977) 24]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1979]]. Quasar was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 24|''Star Wars'' (1977) 24]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 24}} {{1st}} {{Flash}} ==Sources== *{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20070228/index.html|text=Time Capsule: Obi-Wan Kenobi 1979}} *{{CSWECite|III|61|Quasar, Captain}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Quasar }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
